channel.title: +अध्याय १४ खण्डः १-६ on ऋग्वेदे विश्वासटिप्पन्यः
channel.description: Recent content in +अध्याय १४ खण्डः १-६ on ऋग्वेदे विश्वासटिप्पन्यः

item.description:
विश्वास-प्रस्तुतिः

देवा वा असुरैर्युद्धमुपप्रायन्विजयाय ।

English

(On the Origin, Meaning, and Universal Nature of the Agniṣṭoma as the model for other Sacrifices. On the Chatuṣṭoma and Jyotiṣṭoma.)

(On the Origin of the name “Agniṣṭoma”, and its Meaning.)

The Devas went to war with the Asuras, in order to defeat them.

मूलम्

देवा वा असुरैर्युद्धमुपप्रायन्विजयाय ।

विश्वास-प्रस्तुतिः

तानग्निर्नान्वकामयतैतुम् ।

English

Agni was not willing to follow them.

मूलम्

तानग्निर्नान्वकामयतैतुम् ।

विश्वास-प्रस्तुतिः

तं देवा अब्रुवन्नपि त्वमेह्यस्माकं वै त्वमेको ऽसीति ।

विश्वास-प्रस्तुतिः

दीक्षणीयेष्टिस्तायते तामेवानु याः काश्चेष्टयस्ताः सर्वा अग्निष्टोममपियन्ति ।

English

(All Sacrificial Rites are Contained in the Agniṣṭoma.)

When the Dikṣhaṇîya Iṣṭi is once performed in all its parts (lit., is spread), then all other Iṣṭis, whatever they may be, are comprised in the Agniṣṭoma.[2]

English - Notes

[2]The meaning is, the Dikṣaniya Iṣṭi is the model Iṣṭi or prakṛiti, of all the other Iṣṭis required at the Agniṣṭoma, such as the Prâyaṇiya, &c., and is, besides, exactly of the same nature as other independent Iṣṭis, such as the Darśpûrṇima Iṣṭi.

विश्वास-प्रस्तुतिः

इति नु पुरस्तादथोपरिष्टात्पञ्चदशोक्थ्यस्य स्तोत्राणि पञ्चदश शस्त्राणि स मासो मासधा संवत्सरो विहितः ।

English

(The Other Parts of Jyotiṣṭoma, such as Ukthya, Atirātra, Comprised in the Agniṣṭoma.)

Now the first part (of the Agniṣṭoma) has been explained. After that has been performed, the fifteen Stotras and Śastras of the Ukthya ceremony follow. If they (the fifteen Stotras and fifteen Śastras) are taken together, they represent the year as divided into months (each consisting of thirty days).

विश्वास-प्रस्तुतिः

देवा वा असुरैर्विजिग्याना ऊर्ध्वाः स्वर्गं लोकमायन् ।

English

(Why Four Stomas are Required at the Agniṣṭoma.)

The Devas after having (once upon a time) been defeated by the Asuras, started for the celestial world.

मूलम्

देवा वा असुरैर्विजिग्याना ऊर्ध्वाः स्वर्गं लोकमायन् ।

विश्वास-प्रस्तुतिः

सो ऽग्निर्दिविस्पृगूर्ध्व उदश्रयत स स्वर्गस्य लोकस्य द्वारमवृणोदग्निर्वै स्वर्गस्य लोकस्याधिपतिः ।

English

Agni touching the sky (from his place on earth), entered the upper region (with his flames), and closed the gate of the celestial world; for Agni is its master.

<мур>विश्वास-प्रस्तुतिः

स वा एषो ऽग्निरेव यदग्निष्टोमस्तं यदस्तुवंस्तस्मादग्निस्तोमः ।

English

(On the Names “Agniṣṭoma, Chatuṣṭoma, Jyotiṣṭoma.” The Agniṣṭoma is Endless).

The Agniṣṭoma is Agni. It is called so, because they (the gods) praised him with this Stoma.

मूलम्

स वा एषो ऽग्निरेव यदग्निष्टोमस्तं यदस्तुवंस्तस्मादग्निस्तोमः ।

विश्वास-प्रस्तुतिः

तमग्निस्तोमं सन्तमग्निष्टोम इत्याचक्षते परोक्षेण परोक्षप्रिया इव हि देवाः ।

English

They called it so to hide the proper meaning of the word; for the gods like to hide the proper meaning of words.

विश्वास-प्रस्तुतिः

यो वा एष तपत्येषो ऽग्निष्टोम ।

English

(How the Śastras should be Repeated at each of the Three Libations. The Sun never rises nor sets. How the Phenomena of Sunrise and Sunset are to be Explained.)

The Agniṣṭoma is that one who burns (the sun).

मूलम्

यो वा एष तपत्येषो ऽग्निष्टोम ।

विश्वास-प्रस्तुतिः

एष साह्नस्तं सहैवाह्ना संस्थापयेयुः ।

English

The sun shines during the day, and the Agniṣṭoma[23] should be completed along with the day.
